creator: shellingfield@icloud.com
text:
(In reply to Michael Dexter from comment #5)

Hi,

I've usually used BIND9_DLZ as AD DC DNS rather than SAMBA_INTERNAL,
but, Samba Wiki( https://wiki.samba.org/index.php/Samba_Internal_DNS_Back_End ) said
> The internal DNS does not support: 
> shared-key transaction signature (TSIG)

on the other hands, man (samba-)nsupdate said
> -g     This option enables standard GSS-TSIG mode.

then, just my thought,
Samba Internal DNS could not receive DNS Update from nsupdate (-g) via GSS-TSIG
however could receive DNS Update from Windows via called "Secure Dynamic Update"( https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/TSIG ).
though I don't know how difference between them, there seems to be a clear difference.


Regarding dns/samba-nsupdate,
just telling samba stuff to use dns/samba-nsupdate or nsupdate from dns/bind-tools, add below into smb4.conf
  nsupdate command = /usr/local/bin/nsupdate -g
when using BIND9_DLZ as DNS Backend that support GSS-TSIG DNS Update.

'cause man smb4.conf said
>        nsupdate command (G)
>            This option sets the path to the nsupdate command which is used for
>            GSS-TSIG dynamic DNS updates.
> 
>            Default: nsupdate command = /usr/bin/nsupdate -g


> ln -s /usr/local/sbin/samba_dnsupdate /usr/bin/nsupdate
smaba_dnsupdate is a kind of wrapper to call nsupdate or samba-tool, so no need to symlink


in my case, dns/bind918 w/ GSSAPI_HEIMDAL and dns/bind-tools w/ GSSAPI_HEIMDAL or dns/samba-nsupdate w/ GSSAPI_BASE(pkg default) works well like below

# samba_dnsupdate --verbose --all-names
IPs: ['192.168.0.220']
force update: A vm-freebsd.ad.example.com 192.168.0.220
force update: CNAME XXXXXXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXXXXXXXXXX._msdcs.ad.example.com vm-freebsd.ad.example.com
force update: NS ad.example.com vm-freebsd.ad.example.com
force update: NS _msdcs.ad.example.com vm-freebsd.ad.example.com
force update: A ad.example.com 192.168.0.220
force update: SRV _ldap._tcp.ad.example.com vm-freebsd.ad.example.com 389
force update: SRV _ldap._tcp.dc._msdcs.ad.example.com vm-freebsd.ad.example.com 389
force update: SRV _ldap._tcp.XXXXXXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXXXXXXXXXX.domains._msdcs.ad.example.com vm-freebsd.ad.example.com 389
force update: SRV _kerberos._tcp.ad.example.com vm-freebsd.ad.example.com 88
force update: SRV _kerberos._udp.ad.example.com vm-freebsd.ad.example.com 88
force update: SRV _kerberos._tcp.dc._msdcs.ad.example.com vm-freebsd.ad.example.com 88
force update: SRV _kpasswd._tcp.ad.example.com vm-freebsd.ad.example.com 464
force update: SRV _kpasswd._udp.ad.example.com vm-freebsd.ad.example.com 464

(snipped)

force update: SRV _ldap._tcp.Default-First-Site-Name._sites.ForestDnsZones.ad.example.com vm-freebsd.ad.example.com 389
29 DNS updates and 0 DNS deletes needed
Successfully obtained Kerberos ticket to DNS/vm-freebsd.ad.example.com as VM-FREEBSD$
update(nsupdate): A vm-freebsd.ad.example.com 192.168.0.220
Calling nsupdate for A vm-freebsd.ad.example.com 192.168.0.220 (add)
Successfully obtained Kerberos ticket to DNS/vm-freebsd.ad.example.com as VM-FREEBSD$
Outgoing update query:
;; ->>HEADER<<- opcode: UPDATE, status: NOERROR, id:      0
;; flags:; ZONE: 0, PREREQ: 0, UPDATE: 0, ADDITIONAL: 0
;; UPDATE SECTION:
vm-freebsd.ad.example.com. 900  IN      A       192.168.0.220

(snipped)

update(nsupdate): SRV _ldap._tcp.Default-First-Site-Name._sites.ForestDnsZones.ad.example.com vm-freebsd.ad.example.com 389
Calling nsupdate for SRV _ldap._tcp.Default-First-Site-Name._sites.ForestDnsZones.ad.example.com vm-freebsd.ad.example.com 389 (add)
Successfully obtained Kerberos ticket to DNS/vm-freebsd.ad.example.com as VM-FREEBSD$
Outgoing update query:
;; ->>HEADER<<- opcode: UPDATE, status: NOERROR, id:      0
;; flags:; ZONE: 0, PREREQ: 0, UPDATE: 0, ADDITIONAL: 0
;; UPDATE SECTION:
_ldap._tcp.Default-First-Site-Name._sites.ForestDnsZones.ad.example.com. 900 IN SRV 0 100 389 vm-freebsd.ad.example.com.


with above "nsupdate command" line in smb4.conf for several years

Regards,
